fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Utah Occupational Therapist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as an occupational therapist? Here's whether your license transfers to Utah - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: occupational therapist license reciprocity utah, how to transfer occupational therapist license to utah, occupational therapist compact utah, utah occupational therapist endorsement application

board.name: Utah DOPL — Occupational Therapy
board.mailing_address:
Utah Division of Occupational and Professional Licensing
160 East 300 South
Salt Lake City, UT 84111

quick_facts.exam_name: National Board for Certification in Occupational Therapy (NBCOT) examination

license_types.title: Occupational Therapist Registered (OTR)
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice occupational therapy independently in Utah.

license_types.title: Certified Occupational Therapy Assistant (COTA)
license_types.description: Requires an associate degree and practicing under the supervision of a licensed OTR.

steps.title: Create an Account
steps.description: Register or log in to the Utah's Professional Licensing Online System (eGov) to begin your application.

steps.title: Submit Online Application
steps.description: Complete and submit the Occupational Therapist licensure application through the eGov system, paying the associated application fee.

steps.title: Provide Official Documentation
steps.description: Arrange for official transcripts from your ACOTE-accredited occupational therapy program and official NBCOT scores to be sent directly to DOPL.

steps.title: Complete Utah Law and Rules Exam
steps.description: Pass the Utah DOPL Law and Rules Examination, which is typically taken online.

steps.title: Undergo Criminal Background Check
steps.description: Fulfill the criminal background check requirement by submitting fingerprints as instructed by DOPL.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the NBCOT exam.

provisional_text: Utah offers a temporary occupational therapy permit for new graduates who have completed an ACOTE-accredited program and are awaiting their NBCOT exam results. The temporary permit allows practice under the direct supervision of a licensed OTR. Contact the Utah DOPL — Occupational Therapy for current temporary permit requirements and duration limits.
reciprocity_text: Utah is a member of the OT Licensure Compact (OTC). Occupational therapists holding an active compact privilege may practice in Utah without a separate state license. For those not eligible for compact privileges, Utah also accepts standard endorsement applications with proof of current NBCOT certification and license verification from your home state.

faqs.question: How much does a Utah OT license cost?
faqs.answer: The total initial application and licensing fee is approximately $134. Check the board website for the latest fee schedule.

faqs.question: Is NBCOT certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the NBCOT exam is a standard requirement for initial licensure across all states.

faqs.question: Does Utah have reciprocity for occupational therapists?
faqs.answer: Utah has enacted the OT Compact, but compact privileges are not yet issuing. For now, occupational therapists still apply by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my occupational therapist license to Utah?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Utah while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Utah is a member of the OT Licensure Compact (OTC). Occupational therapists holding an active compact privilege may practice in Utah without a separate state license. For those not eligible for compact privileges, Utah also accepts standard endorsement applications with proof of current NBCOT certification and license verification from your home state.

hero.title: Become an Occupational Therapist in Utah
hero.dek: To apply for an Occupational Therapist license in Utah, use the Utah's Professional Licensing Online System (eGov) provided by the Division of Occupational and Professional Licensing (DOPL).

official_links.registration_steps: Visit the Utah's Professional Licensing Online System URL, click 'Create Account' if you don't already have one, then log in and select 'Start a New Application' for Occupational Therapy.
